profile.tagline: GTM strategy for 40+ products. Tech PMM, fluent in GitHub and positioning.
profile.openTo: Senior PMM · Lead PMM · Principal PMM · Head of Product Marketing
bio: Senior Product Marketing Manager with 8+ years of experience in tech. Proven track record in product-led GTM strategy, developer marketing and documentation, and building go-to-market motions across growth-stage and enterprise SaaS.

Background in technical writing means I'm fluent in GitHub, OpenAPI, and developer audiences. Co-founder of Tech Writing BR (Brazil's first and only technical writing community, 300+ members), co-host of The Manu<script> podcast (30+ episodes, guests in 7+ countries).

projects.title: VTEX Vision — 17-Product Launch
projects.problem: VTEX needed to execute its first semiannual global product launch campaign covering 17 products simultaneously, coordinating across engineering, product, design, legal, PR, and external media partners — without a dedicated launch ops function.
projects.solution: Led the full GTM motion end-to-end as sole PMM. Built the launch playbook, coordinated 15+ internal teams and 4 external suppliers, created product messaging for 17 products, developed video scripts and storyboards, managed PR, social, email, and landing page production.
outcomes: 5M+ reach in 30 days
300+ media pieces within 30 days of launch
40+ B2B upsell opps impacted by lead-gen campaign
Awwwards honorable mention for VTEX Vision landing page

projects.title: VTEX Developer Portal
projects.problem: When I joined VTEX as a tech writing intern, our 'Dev Portal' was a Postman collection kept by backend devs. As the product grew to tailor composable experiences, VTEX's developer portal needed to serve 35,000+ developers across 80+ countries with documentation competitive with portals built by companies with significantly more resources — built from scratch.
projects.solution: Co-created an award-winning developer portal from scratch as an open-source project. Documented 50+ REST API endpoints and 30+ API integration guides using OpenAPI/Swagger 3.0. Built content architecture with reading journey design, diagrams, and glossaries. Developed UX Writing guidelines. As Technical Writing Manager, led a team of 4 writers across recruitment, onboarding, mentoring, and quality standards.
outcomes: 35,000+ developers across 80+ countries served monthly
DevPortal Award 2023 — Best Visual Interface (alongside Spotify, Miro, React, Mercedes-Benz)
Also nominated: Best Visual Design, Best API Reference Documentation
5 documentation reader personas from 140 developer survey responses
27+ tools and automations documented and maintained

projects.title: Mobile Feed GTM
projects.problem: iConnections network allocators had no centralized, habit-forming surface in the mobile app. Platform engagement was mainly event-driven and task-oriented.
projects.solution: Defined and executed the full GTM plan for the Mobile Feed — a personalized, social-media-style homepage for allocators. Co-created 200+ content cards with Research and Content teams. Produced the full launch campaign: 5 promotional videos, 5 social media posts, 10 push notifications, and 1 email.
outcomes: 360 unique users in first 35 days
200+ content cards created for launch
43,975 feed session starts at 5 months
24% of LP memberbase reached at 5 months since release— exceeds 10–20% 'established surface' benchmark
~2.3 sessions/user/week — above average for Finance/B2B apps

projects.title: Allocator Pro Loyalty Program
projects.problem: iConnections needed a structured way to increase platform stickiness among its highest-value users — allocators. No loyalty or membership program existed; retention was mostly event-driven, not platform-driven.
projects.solution: Built a tiered membership loyalty program from concept to live product. Owned the full scope, collaborating with Product, Investor Relations and Event Ops to create: tier architecture design, campaign strategy, in-platform experience design, and premium event logistics. Positioned Allocator Pro as a premium access layer linking platform engagement to exclusive in-person benefits.
outcomes: 200 beta members at launch
Built from zero to live product — full end-to-end ownership
Live product + membership management dashboard in platform

projects.title: Platform Weekly Active User Growth
projects.problem: With flagship events across the US, Europe, and Asia attracting over 7,000 investors and fund managers, the iConnections platform and mobile app saw peak usage during event seasons. Off-season retention was the primary challenge, as events drove the majority of platform activity. The goal was to build a structured, year-round lifecycle program to increase baseline platform engagement and reduce reliance on event-driven usage spikes.
projects.solution: Developed ongoing product comms including onboarding emails and push notifications. Created in-app messaging (posts, product tours, banners, surveys) to improve customer education and feature discoverability. Built a 2-month platform-only sales enablement program for client-facing teams — trainings, mock demos, pitch decks, and sell sheets.
outcomes: 61% structural lift in non-event WAU baseline (summer 2024 → summer 2025)
Subscriber WAU grew 19x over the tracked period
53% of users onboarded with new platform emails increased web sessions in first 30 days
Subscriber share of WAU reached 92% (Feb 2026), up from ~56% in July 2024

projects.title: Re-engagement Experiment
projects.problem: A significant cohort of fund managers and allocators had not logged into the platform for 90+ days, considered essentially churned. No prior reengagement strategy existed for this group.
projects.solution: Designed and executed a 4-month structured experiment: 5 campaign hypotheses tested, segmentation framework developed to identify cohorts most likely to respond, cross-referenced open rates, click rates, and platform activity to surface what drove re-engagement for allocators and fund managers.
outcomes: 25.9% of churned customers logged back in
~39,000+ emails delivered across 48 campaigns
5 campaign hypotheses tested over 4 months
~38% reactivation in highest-ROI segment (90–180 day dormant users)
Findings directly informed the 2026 full reengagement program

projects.title: VTEX FastStore GTM
projects.problem: FastStore was VTEX's most technically modern storefront but competed internally against legacy CMS solutions that existing clients already had. Dual positioning challenge: win externally against competing technologies and earn internal recommendation from VTEX sales and CS teams. Real decision-makers were developers.
projects.solution: Developed dual-layer GTM strategy for developer and business buyer audiences. Ran integrated campaign: online activation, sales enablement materials, internal training for commercial teams, and physical activation at VTEX Day (booth + mainstage presentation).
outcomes: Mainstage presentation at VTEX Day
Internal sales and CS alignment program
Enterprise case study: Hearst

projects.title: Tech Writing BR Community
projects.problem: After a few years as a tech writer and podcast host, I kept bumping into the same gap: I was talking to tech writers in Romania, India, the US — but couldn't find my own people in Brazil. Brazilian tech writers were isolated from each other and disconnected from the global practice. There was no local community for professional development, peer support, or networking.
projects.solution: Three of us ran an experiment. We made a Google Slide deck, a logo built on pre-AI design skills, and dropped a calendar invite into the void. We used our personal Google Meet accounts, because really — who was going to show up? The answer was: more people than the platform could hold. Co-founded Tech Writing BR, still the only technical writing community in Brazil, now with volunteers across the country, book clubs, online forums, job board, and ongoing webinars.
outcomes: 300+ members
Only technical writing community in Brazil — 6+ years active
Volunteers across the country, ongoing book clubs and webinars
Book club produced 7 public blog articles translating technical content from English to Portuguese

projects.title: Events & Conferences
projects.problem: Bringing digital product strategy to in-person experiences at scale. Events aren't just logistics — they're the moment your digital messaging has to survive contact with a room full of real people. From AIESEC congresses to iConnections flagship events in Miami, New York, Singapore, and São Paulo, the best product marketing happens when the platform and the physical experience reinforce each other.
projects.solution: Platform pavilions, product demos at scale, in-person campaign activations, and making thousands of allocators feel like they're at the center of something. At VTEX, I led the product demo area at VTEX Day (30,000+ attendees) and the mainstage presentation of FastStore to a non-technical audience. At Global Alts, iConnections flagship event, I led the platform pavilion area design, and structured the user engagement and research activities held in the space. Also led the activation of our loyalty program (7,000+ attendees) blending platform and in-person premium, white-glove service. Earlier in my career, I organized conferences for AIESEC in Brazil and Mexico — including international congresses with 1,000+ attendees.
outcomes: 6 cities across 4 continents (Miami, New York, Singapore, São Paulo, Mexico City, Panama City)
3 product demo areas in global conferences

projects.title: The Manu<script> Podcast
projects.problem: In 2020, tech writing was just starting to surface as a career in Brazil, and I had a question I couldn't answer alone: if what we're doing is called tech writing, how do others actually do it? What can we learn from them to scale what we build? I wanted to talk to tech writers around the world, but I didn't have a platform or an audience. I had to build both from scratch.
projects.solution: So a colleague and I started a podcast. It quickly expanded beyond tech writers to anyone living at the intersection of writing and technology. The Manu<script> became a way to reach professionals across contexts and companies I'd never otherwise access — and to create something of value in exchange for their time. Every episode forced me to deeply research a person's career, industry, and role before we spoke, to understand what they specifically could teach me that no one else could. Interviewing, it turns out, is its own discipline.
outcomes: 30+ episodes published
Guests from 7+ countries (Brazil, USA, UK, India, Romania, Singapore, and others)

projects.title: FrameRoom — Side Project
projects.problem: I feel like a product of our particular zeitgeist: grew up before and after the internet, worked remotely before the pandemic made it normal, built a non-linear career and found it more exciting than a straight line would have been. My current hyperfocus is figuring out how to use AI as an amplifier without letting it replace the things only humans bring. My ceramics and sewing classes are the same instinct in physical form: stay close to making things with your hands. FrameRoom started because my best friend moved into a new place and we wanted to hang posters. Needed a way to visualize gallery wall layouts before committing to nail holes — built for personal use and then shipped. Also an opportunity to explore UX and AI-assisted development.
projects.solution: I wanted to be the human in the loop on the creative decisions — and to use AI to see a project come to life in days. That tension became the product principle. Vibe-coded a browser-based gallery wall planning app built using AI-assisted development. Upload photos of art, remove backgrounds, visualize layouts on a virtual wall, drag-drop until it's right. No account, no install, no server.
outcomes: Shipped as a live app
Open source on GitHub
1 shipped side project demonstrating ability to use AI as a development amplifier
